mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.04% as of 2019-09-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% do not exceed mean returns when FEDFUNDS >= 2.5% by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 3.7% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2019-09-15 remains below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 3.7%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts fall below 1.2 million units annualized, jointly signaling demand weakness. This interaction mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as forward earnings revisions contract.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1.2 million units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 526057M and HOUST at 1364K as of 2019-08-15
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns following joint trigger are not significantly negative (mean return > 0, t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following trigger are positive in > 60% of instances (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month while unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market slack emerging at the margin. This interaction mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns as the market reprices terminal rate expectations and growth outlook.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151722K and UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2019-09-15
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns following trigger are positive in > 55% of instances (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 1.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains above 2.0%, creating real rate tightening that constrains equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns in low-inflation environments.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.358 and FEDFUNDS at 2.04% as of 2019-09-15
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger are positive in > 60% of instances (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts fall below 1.3 million units, jointly signaling demand-side weakness. This interaction mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns as forward earnings estimates contract across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1.3 million units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19021.86B and HOUST at 1364K as of 2019-04-15
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns following trigger are positive in > 55% of instances (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 110.5 (2017=100) while payroll employment grows above 150,000 jobs per month, jointly signaling robust supply-side capacity and labor availability. This interaction mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns as the market reprices growth and inflation expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.92 and PAYEMS at 151722K as of 2019-08-15 and 2019-09-15
regime_hint: robust supply-side expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger are negative in > 55% of instances (binomial test p > 0.10).
